title: papaver rupifragum
intro: Papaver rupifragum, also known as the Himalayan poppy or blue mecon, is a species of flowering plant in the poppy family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of coughs, respiratory issues, and skin problems. The roots and seeds of Papaver rupifragum contain alkaloids such as meconic acid and meconidine, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, offering potential health benefits in reducing congestion and soothing irritated skin. In herbal preparations, the roots and seeds are typically dried and used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, although more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of these uses.
